description_long: This is a robust axe featuring a broad, roughly hewn metal blade with a slightly irregular edge, suggesting it is designed for heavy-duty chopping or splitting tasks. The blade is attached to a thick, rectangular wooden handle that is segmented into three distinct sections, each separated by squared-off bands, providing a secure grip and added structural integrity. The wood appears weathered and textured, with a matte finish that contrasts with the metallic sheen of the blade. The blade itself is wide and flat, with a pronounced poll and a straight cutting edge, lacking any decorative elements or branding. The mounting of the blade to the handle is traditional, with the metal head fitted snugly onto the squared end of the handle. This utilitarian design is typical of axes found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A heavy-duty axe with a broad, rough metal blade and a segmented, rectangular wooden handle, designed for robust chopping tasks. The utilitarian design features a matte wood grip and a wide, flat blade.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the wide metal blade and the segmented wooden handle.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the rectangular cross-section of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's flat face and the handle's squared segments.
description_view_3: Front view showing the blade's edge and the handle's alignment with the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Rectangular, segmented wooden handle with squared grip sections, straight and robust, matte finish.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, flat, single-edged steel blade with a straight edge and pronounced poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the squared end of the handle.
